target.name: Majestic Labs
target.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Majestic Labs is a Series A semiconductor company that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip companies, with primary reliance on 4 same-stage private peers: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the company's $100M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $200M. Peer-median triangulation across the four same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public comps (median P/S of 24.2x) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue, which private companies rarely provide.

The triangulated valuation range is $170M to $1.7B, with a median of $850M. This wide range reflects genuine uncertainty in early-stage frontier semiconductors where comparable companies span a 10x valuation spread. Stage-progression yields a more conservative view ($200M), while peer-median anchors closer to the upper quartile of recent private market activity. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to peers, capital efficiency, and path to meaningful revenue—factors not quantifiable from available data.
